title: Alberta Introduces $200 Annual Tax on Electric Vehicles
content: Starting February 13, Albertans will be required to pay a $200 annual tax when registering their electric vehicles (EVs), aligning with the fuel tax paid by gas-powered vehicle owners. The fee will be collected by registry agents and MyAlberta eService. Hybrid vehicles, electric motorcycles, and off-highway EVs are exempt. Officials, including Finance Minister Nate Horner and Service Alberta Minister Dale Nally, emphasized fairness in road maintenance contributions. The tax was part of Budget 2024 and passed under the Financial Statutes Amendment Act. Exemptions apply for federal agencies, diplomats, and First Nations individuals living on reserves.
author: Analysts Account
